free_text: Fast and slow processes in the fragmentation of U-238
by 85 MeV/nucleon C-12.
- The reaction of 86 MeV/nucleon C-12 with Au-197 and
details of experiment.
Two thin U-targets consisting of evaporated deposits
of UF(4) on a 13.4-mg/cm**2 Al-backing, were used.
The 'forward' and 'backward' U deposits thicknesses
were determined by alpha counting to be 783 and 804-
mug/cm**2 , respectively.
*By authors*.The catcher foils geometry was identical
to that used previously except that the backward angle
catchers subtending 143-153-degree and 153-166-degree
were combined into a single catcher subtending 143-
166-degree.
.Firestreak model. Hydrodynamical model.
The laboratory-frame angular distributions were
transformed into the moving frame using various
assumptions about the moving frame velocity.
CERN SC synchrocyclotron
Off-line gamma-ray spectroscopy
No information about source of uncertainties
Uncertainty in monitor reaction
